findings.description: The original PDF uses a centered header layout with dotted leader lines connecting topics to speakers, while the HTML converts the agenda into a structured table format.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a centered title block and agenda items with dotted leaders connecting topics to speakers in a free-form layout. HTML renders the same content in a formal table with Time, Topic, and Speaker columns.

findings.description: The HTML introduces a formal table structure where the PDF had no table — but this is a reasonable semantic conversion that preserves all data and relationships correctly.
findings.evidence: PDF uses dotted leaders to associate topics with speakers. HTML uses a 3-column table (Time, Topic, Speaker) with all data correctly placed.

findings.description: Title formatting differs: PDF has bold centered text for the title, HTML uses larger left-aligned heading style with a horizontal rule underneath.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'New and Watauga Hydrologic Model' and 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' are bold, centered, same-size text. HTML: These appear as large left-aligned headings with a dark horizontal rule below.

findings.description: The PDF presents the agenda as a formatted list with dotted leaders, not as a table with explicit columns. The HTML converts this into a structured table with 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Speaker' column headers. While the data is accurate, the column headers 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Speaker' are fabricated labels not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: The PDF has no table headers. The HTML adds <th>Time</th>, <th>Topic</th>, <th>Speaker</th> which are inferred but not explicitly stated in the source.

findings.description: The HTML contains two nested <main> elements, though this is a structural HTML issue rather than content duplication. No actual content is duplicated.
